children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: There is hereby imposed a tax on the failure of a group health plan to meet the requirements of subsection (f) with respect to any qualified beneficiary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a) on any failure with respect to a qualified beneficiary shall be $100 for each day in the noncompliance period with respect to such failure.

children.label: A
children.text: beginning on the date such failure first occurs, and

children.label: i
children.text: the date such failure is corrected, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date which is 6 months after the last day in the period applicable to the qualified beneficiary under subsection (f)(2)(B) (determined without regard to clause (iii) thereof).

children.heading: Minimum tax for noncompliance period where failure discovered after notice of examination
children.label: 3 Minimum tax for noncompliance period where failure discovered after notice of examination
i
children.text: which are not corrected before the date a notice of examination of income tax liability is sent to the employer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which occurred or continued during the period under examination,

children.heading: Higher minimum tax where violations are more than de minimis
children.label: B Higher minimum tax where violations are more than de minimis
children.text: To the extent violations by the employer (or the plan in the case of a multiemployer plan) for any year are more than de minimis, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting “$15,000” for “$2,500” with respect to the employer (or such plan).

children.heading: Limitations on amount of tax
children.label: c Limitations on amount of tax

children.heading: Tax not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.label: 1 Tax not to apply where failure not discovered exercising reasonable diligence
children.text: No tax shall be imposed by subsection (a) on any failure during any period for which it is established to the satisfaction of the Secretary that none of the persons referred to in subsection (e) knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: Tax not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
children.label: 2 Tax not to apply to failures corrected within 30 days
A
children.text: such failure was due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, and

children.label: B
children.text: such failure is corrected during the 30-day period beginning on the 1st date any of the persons referred to in subsection (e) knew, or exercising reasonable diligence would have known, that such failure existed.

children.heading: $100 limit on amount of tax for failures on any day with respect to a qualified beneficiary
children.label: 3 $100 limit on amount of tax for failures on any day with respect to a qualified beneficiary

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the maximum amount of tax imposed by subsection (a) on failures on any day during the noncompliance period with respect to a qualified beneficiary shall be $100.

children.heading: Special rule where more than 1 qualified beneficiary
children.label: B Special rule where more than 1 qualified beneficiary
children.text: If there is more than 1 qualified beneficiary with respect to the same qualifying event, the maximum amount of tax imposed by subsection (a) on all failures on any day during the noncompliance period with respect to such qualified beneficiaries shall be $200.

children.label: I
children.text: 10 percent of the aggregate amount paid or incurred by the employer (or predecessor employer) during the preceding taxable year for group health plans, or

children.heading: Taxable years in the case of certain controlled groups
children.label: ii Taxable years in the case of certain controlled groups
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, if not all persons who are treated as a single employer for purposes of this section have the same taxable year, the taxable years taken into account shall be determined under principles similar to the principles of section 1561.

children.label: I
children.text: 10 percent of the amount paid or incurred by such trust during such taxable year to provide medical care (as defined in section 213(d)) directly or through insurance, reimbursement, or otherwise, or

children.heading: Special rule for employers required to pay tax
children.label: ii Special rule for employers required to pay tax
children.text: If an employer is assessed a tax imposed by subsection (a) by reason of a failure with respect to a multiemployer plan, the limit shall be determined under subparagraph (A) (and not under this subparagraph) and as if such plan were not a multiemployer plan.

children.heading: Special rule for persons providing benefits
children.label: C Special rule for persons providing benefits
children.text: In the case of a person described in subsection (e)(1)(B) (and not subsection (e)(1)(A)), the aggregate amount of tax imposed by subsection (a) for failures during a taxable year with respect to all plans shall not exceed $2,000,000.

children.heading: Waiver by Secretary
children.label: 5 Waiver by Secretary
children.text: In the case of a failure which is due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, the Secretary may waive part or all of the tax imposed by subsection (a) to the extent that the payment of such tax would be excessive relative to the failure involved.

children.heading: Tax not to apply to certain plans
children.label: d Tax not to apply to certain plans

children.text: any failure of a group health plan to meet the requirements of subsection (f) with respect to any qualified beneficiary if the qualifying event with respect to such beneficiary occurred during the calendar year immediately following a calendar year during which all employers maintaining such plan normally employed fewer than 20 employees on a typical business day,
any governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)), or
any church plan (within the meaning of section 414(e)).

children.label: i
children.text: In the case of a plan other than a multiemployer plan, the employer.

children.label: ii
children.text: In the case of a multiemployer plan, the plan.

children.label: B
children.text: Each person who is responsible (other than in a capacity as an employee) for administering or providing benefits under the plan and whose act or failure to act caused (in whole or in part) the failure.

children.heading: Special rules for persons described in paragraph (1)(B)
children.label: 2 Special rules for persons described in paragraph (1)(B)

children.heading: No liability unless written agreement
children.label: A No liability unless written agreement
children.text: Except in the case of liability resulting from the application of subparagraph (B) of this paragraph, a person described in subparagraph (B) (and not in subparagraph (A)) of paragraph (1) shall be liable for the tax imposed by subsection (a) on any failure only if such person assumed (under a legally enforceable written agreement) responsibility for the performance of the act to which the failure relates.

children.heading: Failure to cover qualified beneficiaries where current employees are covered
children.label: B Failure to cover qualified beneficiaries where current employees are covered
i

children.heading: Continuation coverage requirements of group health plans
children.label: f Continuation coverage requirements of group health plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A group health plan meets the requirements of this subsection only if the coverage of the costs of pediatric vaccines (as defined under section 1928(h)(6) of the Social Security Act ()) is not reduced below the coverage provided by the plan as of , and only if each qualified beneficiary who would lose coverage under the plan as a result of a qualifying event is entitled to elect, within the election period, continuation coverage under the plan.42 U.S.C. 1396s(h)(6)May 1, 1993

children.heading: Type of benefit coverage
children.label: A Type of benefit coverage
children.text: The coverage must consist of coverage which, as of the time the coverage is being provided, is identical to the coverage provided under the plan to similarly situated beneficiaries under the plan with respect to whom a qualifying event has not occurred. If coverage under the plan is modified for any group of similarly situated beneficiaries, the coverage shall also be modified in the same manner for all individuals who are qualified beneficiaries under the plan pursuant to this subsection in connection with such group.

children.heading: General rule for terminations and reduced hours
children.label: I General rule for terminations and reduced hours
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B), except as provided in subclause (II), the date which is 18 months after the date of the qualifying event.

children.heading: Special rule for multiple qualifying events
children.label: II Special rule for multiple qualifying events
children.text: If a qualifying event (other than a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(F)) occurs during the 18 months after the date of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B), the date which is 36 months after the date of the qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B).

children.heading: Special rule for certain bankruptcy proceedings
children.label: III Special rule for certain bankruptcy proceedings
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(F) (relating to bankruptcy proceedings), the date of the death of the covered employee or qualified beneficiary (described in subsection (g)(1)(D)(iii)), or in the case of the surviving spouse or dependent children of the covered employee, 36 months after the date of the death of the covered employee.

children.heading: General rule for other qualifying events
children.label: IV General rule for other qualifying events
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event not described in paragraph (3)(B) or (3)(F), the date which is 36 months after the date of the qualifying event.

children.heading: Special rule for PBGC recipients
children.label: V Special rule for PBGC recipients
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B) with respect to a covered employee who (as of such qualifying event) has a nonforfeitable right to a benefit any portion of which is to be paid by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation under title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, notwithstanding subclause (I) or (II), the date of the death of the covered employee, or in the case of the surviving spouse or dependent children of the covered employee, 24 months after the date of the death of the covered employee. The preceding sentence shall not require any period of coverage to extend beyond .January 1, 2014

children.heading: Special rule for TAA-eligible individuals
children.label: VI Special rule for TAA-eligible individuals
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B) with respect to a covered employee who is (as of the date that the period of coverage would, but for this subclause or subclause (VII), otherwise terminate under subclause (I) or (II)) a TAA-eligible individual (as defined in paragraph (5)(C)(iv)(II)), the period of coverage shall not terminate by reason of subclause (I) or (II), as the case may be, before the later of the date specified in such subclause or the date on which such individual ceases to be such a TAA-eligible individual. The preceding sentence shall not require any period of coverage to extend beyond .January 1, 2014

children.heading: Medicare entitlement followed by qualifying event
children.label: VII Medicare entitlement followed by qualifying event
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in paragraph (3)(B) that occurs less than 18 months after the date the covered employee became entitled to benefits under title XVIII of the Social Security Act, the period of coverage for qualified beneficiaries other than the covered employee shall not terminate under this clause before the close of the 36-month period beginning on the date the covered employee became so entitled.

children.heading: Special rule for disability
children.label: VIII Special rule for disability
children.text: In the case of a qualified beneficiary who is determined, under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act, to have been disabled at any time during the first 60 days of continuation coverage under this section, any reference in subclause (I) or (II) to 18 months is deemed a reference to 29 months (with respect to all qualified beneficiaries), but only if the qualified beneficiary has provided notice of such determination under paragraph (6)(C) before the end of such 18 months.

children.heading: End of plan
children.label: ii End of plan
children.text: The date on which the employer ceases to provide any group health plan to any employee.

children.heading: Failure to pay premium
children.label: iii Failure to pay premium
children.text: The date on which coverage ceases under the plan by reason of a failure to make timely payment of any premium required under the plan with respect to the qualified beneficiary. The payment of any premium (other than any payment referred to in the last sentence of subparagraph (C)) shall be considered to be timely if made within 30 days after the date due or within such longer period as applies to or under the plan.

children.heading: Group health plan coverage or medicare entitlement
children.label: iv Group health plan coverage or medicare entitlement
I
children.text: covered under any other group health plan (as an employee or otherwise) which does not contain any exclusion or limitation with respect to any preexisting condition of such beneficiary (other than such an exclusion or limitation which does not apply to (or is satisfied by) such beneficiary by reason of chapter 100 of this title, part 7 of subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, or title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act), or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a qualified beneficiary other than a qualified beneficiary described in subsection (g)(1)(D) entitled to benefits under title XVIII of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: Termination of extended coverage for disability
children.label: v Termination of extended coverage for disability
children.text: In the case of a qualified beneficiary who is disabled at any time during the first 60 days of continuation coverage under this section, the month that begins more than 30 days after the date of the final determination under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act that the qualified beneficiary is no longer disabled.

children.text: may, at the election of the payor, be made in monthly installments.

children.heading: No requirement of insurability
children.label: D No requirement of insurability
children.text: The coverage may not be conditioned upon, or discriminate on the basis of lack of, evidence of insurability.

children.heading: Conversion option
children.label: E Conversion option
children.text: In the case of a qualified beneficiary whose period of continuation coverage expires under subparagraph (B)(i), the plan must, during the 180-day period ending on such expiration date, provide to the qualified beneficiary the option of enrollment under a conversion health plan otherwise generally available under the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: The death of the covered employee.

children.label: B
children.text: The termination (other than by reason of such employee’s gross misconduct), or reduction of hours, of the covered employee’s employment.

children.label: C
children.text: The divorce or legal separation of the covered employee from the employee’s spouse.

children.label: D
children.text: The covered employee becoming entitled to benefits under title XVIII of the Social Security Act.

children.label: E
children.text: A dependent child ceasing to be a dependent child under the generally applicable requirements of the plan.

children.label: F
children.text: A proceeding in a case under title 11, United States Code, commencing on or after , with respect to the employer from whose employment the covered employee retired at any time.July 1, 1986

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “applicable premium” means, with respect to any period of continuation coverage of qualified beneficiaries, the cost to the plan for such period of the coverage for similarly situated beneficiaries with respect to whom a qualifying event has not occurred (without regard to whether such cost is paid by the employer or employee).

children.label: B Special rule for self-insured plans
I
children.text: is determined on an actuarial basis, and

children.label: II
children.text: takes into account such factors as the Secretary may prescribe in regulations.

children.heading: Determination on basis of past cost
children.label: ii Determination on basis of past cost
I
children.text: the cost to the plan for similarly situated beneficiaries for the same period occurring during the preceding determination period under subparagraph (C), adjusted by

children.label: II
children.text: the percentage increase or decrease in the implicit price deflator of the gross national product (calculated by the Department of Commerce and published in the Survey of Current Business) for the 12-month period ending on the last day of the sixth month of such preceding determination period.

children.heading: Clause (ii) not to apply where significant change
children.label: iii Clause (ii) not to apply where significant change
children.text: A plan administrator may not elect to have clause (ii) apply in any case in which there is any significant difference between the determination period and the preceding determination period, in coverage under, or in employees covered by, the plan. The determination under the preceding sentence for any determination period shall be made at the same time as the determination under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Determination period
children.label: C Determination period
children.text: The determination of any applicable premium shall be made for a period of 12 months and shall be made before the beginning of such period.

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any qualified beneficiary who receives notice under paragraph (6)(D), the date of such notice.

children.heading: Effect of election on other beneficiaries
children.label: B Effect of election on other beneficiaries
children.text: Except as otherwise specified in an election, any election of continuation coverage by a qualified beneficiary described in subparagraph (A)(i) or (B) of subsection (g)(1) shall be deemed to include an election of continuation coverage on behalf of any other qualified beneficiary who would lose coverage under the plan by reason of the qualifying event. If there is a choice among types of coverage under the plan, each qualified beneficiary is entitled to make a separate selection among such types of coverage.

children.heading: Temporary extension of COBRA election period for certain individuals
children.label: C Temporary extension of COBRA election period for certain individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a nonelecting TAA-eligible individual and notwithstanding subparagraph (A), such individual may elect continuation coverage under this subsection during the 60-day period that begins on the first day of the month in which the individual becomes a TAA-eligible individual, but only if such election is made not later than 6 months after the date of the TAA-related loss of coverage.

children.heading: Commencement of coverage; no reach-back
children.label: ii Commencement of coverage; no reach-back
children.text: Any continuation coverage elected by a TAA-eligible individual under clause (i) shall commence at the beginning of the 60-day election period described in such paragraph and shall not include any period prior to such 60-day election period.

children.label: I
children.text: beginning on the date of the TAA-related loss of coverage, and

children.label: II
children.text: ending on the first day of the 60-day election period described in clause (i),

children.heading: Nonelecting TAA-eligible individual
children.label: I Nonelecting TAA-eligible individual
children.text: The term “nonelecting TAA-eligible individual” means a TAA-eligible individual who has a TAA-related loss of coverage and did not elect continuation coverage under this subsection during the TAA-related election period.

children.heading: TAA-eligible individual
children.label: II TAA-eligible individual
children.text: The term “TAA-eligible individual” means an eligible TAA recipient (as defined in paragraph (2) of section 35(c)) and an eligible alternative TAA recipient (as defined in paragraph (3) of such section).

children.heading: TAA-related election period
children.label: III TAA-related election period
children.text: The term “TAA-related election period” means, with respect to a TAA-related loss of coverage, the 60-day election period under this subsection which is a direct consequence of such loss.

children.heading: TAA-related loss of coverage
children.label: IV TAA-related loss of coverage
children.text: The term “TAA-related loss of coverage” means, with respect to an individual whose separation from employment gives rise to being an TAA-eligible individual, the loss of health benefits coverage associated with such separation.

children.label: A
children.text: The group health plan shall provide, at the time of commencement of coverage under the plan, written notice to each covered employee and spouse of the employee (if any) of the rights provided under this subsection.

children.label: B
children.text: The employer of an employee under a plan must notify the plan administrator of a qualifying event described in subparagraph (A), (B), (D), or (F) of paragraph (3) with respect to such employee within 30 days (or, in the case of a group health plan which is a multiemployer plan, such longer period of time as may be provided in the terms of the plan) of the date of the qualifying event.

children.label: C
children.text: Each covered employee or qualified beneficiary is responsible for notifying the plan administrator of the occurrence of any qualifying event described in subparagraph (C) or (E) of paragraph (3) within 60 days after the date of the qualifying event and each qualified beneficiary who is determined, under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act, to have been disabled at any time during the first 60 days of continuation coverage under this section is responsible for notifying the plan administrator of such determination within 60 days after the date of the determination and for notifying the plan administrator within 30 days of the date of any final determination under such title or titles that the qualified beneficiary is no longer disabled.

children.label: 7 Covered employee
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “covered employee” means an individual who is (or was) provided coverage under a group health plan by virtue of the performance of services by the individual for 1 or more persons maintaining the plan (including as an employee defined in section 401(c)(1)).

children.heading: Special rule for terminations and reduced employment
children.label: B Special rule for terminations and reduced employment
children.text: In the case of a qualifying event described in subsection (f)(3)(B), the term “qualified beneficiary” includes the covered employee.

children.heading: Exception for nonresident aliens
children.label: C Exception for nonresident aliens
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A) and (B), the term “qualified beneficiary” does not include an individual whose status as a covered employee is attributable to a period in which such individual was a nonresident alien who received no earned income (within the meaning of section 911(d)(2)) from the employer which constituted income from sources within the United States (within the meaning of section 861(a)(3)). If an individual is not a qualified beneficiary pursuant to the previous sentence, a spouse or dependent child of such individual shall not be considered a qualified beneficiary by virtue of the relationship of the individual.

children.heading: Special rule for retirees and widows
children.label: D Special rule for retirees and widows
i
children.text: as the spouse of the covered employee,

children.label: ii
children.text: as the dependent child of the covered employee, or

children.label: iii
children.text: as the surviving spouse of the covered employee.

children.heading: Group health plan
children.label: 2 Group health plan
children.text: The term “group health plan” has the meaning given such term by section 5000(b)(1). Such term shall not include any plan substantially all of the coverage under which is for qualified long-term care services (as defined in section 7702B(c)).

children.heading: Plan administrator
children.label: 3 Plan administrator
children.text: The term “plan administrator” has the meaning given the term “administrator” by section 3(16)(A) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.
